Two issues:
1. `uv` is missing (optional, but preferred)
2. `psycopg-binary==3.2.7` isn't available for Python 3.14; only newer versions are available

---


The issue is that `psycopg-binary==3.2.7` isn't available for Python 3.14. The requirements file pins 3.2.7 (with a note that 3.2.8 causes test failures), but only 3.2.10+ are available for Python 3.14.

Checking for Python version requirements or documentation:

The project targets Python 3.10+, but Python 3.14 doesn't have `psycopg-binary==3.2.7`. Updating `psycopg-binary` to a version compatible with Python 3.14. Since 3.2.8 is noted to cause test failures, using 3.2.10 (the minimum available for Python 3.14) to stay in the 3.2.x series.

---


Updated `psycopg-binary` to 3.2.10 for Python 3.14 compatibility. Run `bin/environmentd` again; it should work.

The script will use `pip` if `uv` isn't installed. For faster dependency resolution, you can install `uv` with:
```bash
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
```

This is optional; the script works without it.

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_


`scipy==1.15.3` is building from source because there's no pre-built wheel for Python 3.14, and it requires a Fortran compiler (gfortran) that isn't installed. Checking scipy compatibility and whether we can use a newer version or install the compiler:

[SciPy 1.14.1 Release Notes #](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy-1.15.3/release/1.14.1-notes.html)<br/>3. [Toolchain Roadmap #](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy-1.15.3/dev/toolchain.html)<br/>4. [Toolchain Roadmap #](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/dev/toolchain.html)<br/><br/># SciPy 1.15.3 and Python 3.14 Compatibility<br/><br/>Based on the available information, **SciPy 1.15.3 does not support Python 3.14**. According to the toolchain roadmap, SciPy 1.15 supports Python versions >=3.10, <3.13.[5] This means SciPy 1.15.3 is compatible with Python 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12, but not Python 3.14.<br/><br/>SciPy 1.15.3 is a bug-fix release with no new features compared to 1.15.2.[1] For Python 3.13 support, you would need SciPy 1.14.1 or later, which added support for Python 3.13 including binary wheels on PyPI.[2]<br/><br/>If you need Python 3.14 compatibility, you would need to wait for a newer SciPy minor version (such as 1.16 or later) that officially supports Python 3.14. |

        "contents": "# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors.     {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./deny.toml",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 48
          }
        },
        "contents": "[graph]\ntargets = [\n    \"x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu\",\n    \"aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu\",\n    \"aarch64-apple-darwin\",\n]\n\n[bans]\nmultiple-versions = \"deny\"\n# Try to avoid exemptions for duplicate dependencies! Duplicate dependencies\n# slow down compilation, bloat the binary, and tickle race conditions in &#96;cargo\n# doc&#96; (see rust-lang/cargo#3613).\n#\n# If possible, submit PRs upstream to remove duplicated transitive dependencies.\n# You can use patch directives in the root Cargo.toml to point at a\n# Materialize-maintained fork that avoids the duplicated transitive\n# dependencies.\nskip = [\n    # arrayvec had a significant API change in 0.7\n    { name = \"arrayvec\", version = \"0.5.2\" },\n    # One-time exception for base64 due to its prevalence in the crate graph.\n    { name = \"base64\", version = \"0.13.1\" },\n    # &#96;syn&#96; is a core crate that a huge part of the ecosystem either directly, or\n    # transitively depends on. They just released v2.0 which not all crates have\n    # migrated to yet.\n    { name = \"syn\", version = \"1.0.107\" },\n    # &#96;tokio&#96; depends on a newer version of socket2, we are okay with _temporarily_\n    # allowing these duplicate dependencies until the rest of the ecosystem catches\n    # up.\n    { name = \"socket2\", version = \"0.4.9\" },\n    # held back by thrift 0.17.0 depended on by parquet 51.0.0\n    { name = \"ordered-float\", version = \"2.10.1\" },\n    { name = \"ordered-float\", version = \"4.6.0\" },\n    # held back by 'ciborium' which is depended on by 'criterion'\n    { name = \"half\", version = \"1.6.0\" },\n\n    # Will require updating many crates\n    { name = \"indexmap\", version = \"1.9.1\" },\n    # Required by indexmap 1.9.1, which is depended on by many things\n    { name = \"hashbrown\", version = \"0.12.3\" },\n    { name = \"hashbrown\", version = \"0.14.5\" },\n    # Required by hashbrown\n    { name = \"foldhash\", version = \"0.1.5\" },\n\n    # Had to update &#96;tower-http&#96; to &#96;0.4.3&#96; to fix the &#96;base64&#96; duplicate version\n    # but this introduced a new dependency on &#96;bitflags 2.3.3&#96; but all the rest of\n    # our crates use &#96;bitflags 1.3.2&#96;\n    # TODO: fork &#96;tower-http&#96; and swap to use older bitflags",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "[graph]",
            "lineNumber": 1

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "[package]\nname = \"mz-compute\"\ndescription = \"Materialize's compute layer.\"\nversion = \"0.0.0\"\nedition.workspace = true\nrust-version.workspace = true\npublish = false\n\n[lints]\nworkspace = true\n\n[dependencies]\nanyhow = \"1.0.100\"\nasync-stream = \"0.3.6\"\nbytesize = \"2.1.0\"\ncolumnar = \"0.11.1\"\ndec = { version = \"0.4.8\", features = [\"serde\"] }\ndifferential-dataflow = \"0.18.0\"\ndifferential-dogs3 = \"0.18.0\"\nfail = { version = \"0.5.1\", features = [\"failpoints\"] }\nfutures = \"0.3.31\"\nitertools = \"0.14.0\"\nlgalloc = \"0.6\"\nmz-cluster = { path = \"../cluster\" }\nmz-cluster-client = { path = \"../cluster-client\" }\nmz-compute-client = { path = \"../compute-client\" }\nmz-compute-types = { path = \"../compute-types\" }\nmz-dyncfg = { path = \"../dyncfg\" }\nmz-dyncfgs = { path = \"../dyncfgs\" }\nmz-expr = { path = \"../expr\" }\nmz-metrics = { path = \"../metrics\" }\nmz-ore = { path = \"../ore\", features = [\"async\", \"process\", \"tracing\", \"columnar\", \"differential-dataflow\", \"region\"] }\nmz-persist-client = { path = \"../persist-client\" }\nmz-persist-types = { path = \"../persist-types\" }\nmz-repr = { path = \"../repr\" }\nmz-storage-operators = { path = \"../storage-operators\" }\nmz-storage-types = { path = \"../storage-types\" }\nmz-timely-util = { path = \"../timely-util\" }\nmz-txn-wal = { path = \"../txn-wal\" }\nprometheus = { version = \"0.14.0\", default-features = false }\nscopeguard = \"1.2.0\"\nserde = { version = \"1.0.219\", features = [\"derive\"] }\nsmallvec = { version = \"1.15.1\", features = [\"serde\", \"union\"] }\ntimely = \"0.25.1\"\ntokio = { version = \"1.48.0\", features = [\"fs\", \"rt\", \"sync\", \"net\"] }\ntracing = \"0.1.44\"\nuuid = { version = \"1.19.0\", features = [\"serde\", \"v4\"] }\nworkspace-hack = { version = \"0.0.0\", path = \"../workspace-hack\", optional = true }\n\n[target.'cfg(not(target_os = \"macos\"))'.dependencies]\ncore_affinity = \"0.8.3\"\n\n[package.metadata.cargo-udeps.ignore]\nnormal = [\"workspace-hack\"]\n\n[features]\ndefault = [\"workspace-hack\"]",

        "contents": "[package]\nname = \"mz-repr\"\ndescription = \"The core data types for Materialize.\"\nversion = \"0.0.0\"\nedition.workspace = true\nrust-version.workspace = true\npublish = false\n\n[lints]\nworkspace = true\n\n[[bench]]\nname = \"row\"\nharness = false\n\n[[bench]]\nname = \"strconv\"\nharness = false\n\n[[bench]]\nname = \"packed\"\nharness = false\n\n[[bench]]\nname = \"data_types\"\nharness = false\n\n[dependencies]\nanyhow = \"1.0.100\"\narrow = { version = \"57\", default-features = false }\nbitflags = \"1.3.2\"\nbytemuck = { version = \"1.23.1\", features = [\"latest_stable_rust\"] }\nbytes = \"1.10.1\"\ncfg-if = \"1.0.4\"\ncolumnar = \"0.11.1\"\ncolumnation = \"0.1.0\"\nchrono = { version = \"0.4.39\", default-features = false, features = [\"serde\", \"std\"] }\ncompact_bytes = \"0.2.1\"\ndec = \"0.4.8\"\ndifferential-dataflow = \"0.18.0\"\nenum-kinds = \"0.5.1\"\nhex = \"0.4.3\"\nitertools = \"0.14.0\"\nmz-lowertest = { path = \"../lowertest\", default-features = false }\nmz-ore = { path = \"../ore\", features = [\n    \"bytes\",\n    \"id_gen\",\n    \"smallvec\",\n    \"region\",\n    \"stack\",\n    \"test\",\n    \"tracing\",\n], default-features = false }\nmz-persist-types = { path = \"../persist-types\", default-features = false }\nmz-pgtz = { path = \"../pgtz\", default-features = false }\nmz-proto = { path = \"../proto\", features = [\"chrono\"], default-features = false }\nmz-sql-parser = { path = \"../sql-parser\", default-features = false }\nmz-timely-util = { path = \"../timely-util\", default-features = false }\nnum-traits = \"0.2.15\"\nnum_enum = \"0.7.5\"\nordered-float = { version = \"5.1.0\", features = [\"serde\"] }\npostgres-protocol = { version = \"0.6.9\" }\nprost = { version = \"0.13.5\", features = [\"no-recursion-limit\"] }\nregex = \"1.12.2\"\nryu = \"1.0.22\"\nserde = { version = \"1.0.219\", features = [\"derive\"] }\nserde_json = { version = \"1.0.149\", features = \"0.25.1\"\ntokio-postgres = { version = \"0.7.15\" }\ntracing-core = \"0.1.35\"\nurl = { version = \"2.5.8\", features = [\"serde\"] }",

        "contents": "# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n#\n# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n# You may obtain a copy of the License in the LICENSE file at the\n# root of this repository, or online at\n#\n#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n#\n# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n# distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n# limitations under the License.\n\n[project]\nname = \"mcp_materialize_agents\"\nversion = \"0.3.2\"\ndescription = \"Live data layer for agents. Expose canonical business objects as database-consistent, instantly-queryable SQL views via Model Context Protocol\"\nreadme = \"README.md\"\nrequires-python = \"&gt;=3.10\"\nauthors = [\n    {name = \"Materialize Inc\", email = \"support@materialize.com\"}\n]\n\nkeywords = [\"materialize\", \"mcp\", \"database\", \"api\"]\ndependencies = [\n    \"mcp&gt;=1.10.1\",\n    \"psycopg-pool&gt;=3.2.6\",\n    \"psycopg[binary]&gt;=3.2.6\",\n    \"pydantic&gt;=2.0.0\",\n]\n\n[project.scripts]\nmcp_materialize_agents = \"mcp_materialize_agents.mcp_materialize_agents:main\"\n\n[build-system]\nrequires = [\"hatchling\"]\nbuild-backend = \"hatchling.build\"\n\n[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]\npackages = [\"mcp_materialize_agents\"]\n\n[tool.hatch.build]\n\n[dependency-groups]\ndev = [\n    \"pytest-asyncio&gt;=0.26.0\",\n    \"pytest&gt;=8.3.5\",\n    \"hatch&gt;=1.9.4\"\n]\n\n[tool.ruff]\nline-length = 88\nlint.select = [\"I\", \"UP\", \"F\", \"E\"]\nlint.extend-select = [\"E501\"]",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/ore/Cargo.toml",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 47

        },
        "contents": "[package]\nname = \"mz-ore\"\ndescription = \"Internal utility libraries for Materialize.\"\nversion = \"0.1.0\"\nlicense = \"Apache-2.0\"\nedition.workspace = true\nrust-version.workspace = true\nauthors = [\"Materialize, Inc.\"]\n\n[lints]\nworkspace = true\n\n[dependencies]\nanyhow = { version = \"1.0.100\", optional = true }\n# NB: ore is meant to be an extension of the Rust stdlib. To keep it\n# lightweight, dependencies on external crates should be avoided if possible. If\n# an external crate is required, it must be optional and feature-gated.\n#\n# The only exception to the above is the &#96;either&#96; crate, which itself has zero\n# dependencies and is widely considered to be basically part of the stdlib.\nasync-trait = { version = \"0.1.89\", optional = true }\nbytemuck = { version = \"1.23.1\", optional = true }\nbytes = { version = \"1.10.1\", optional = true }\nchrono = { version = \"0.4.39\", default-features = false, features = [\"std\"], optional = true }\nclap = { version = \"4.5.23\", features = [\"env\", \"string\"], optional = true }\ncolumnation = { version = \"0.1.0\", optional = true }\ncolumnar = { version = \"0.11.1\", optional = true }\ncompact_bytes = { version = \"0.2.1\", optional = true }\nctor = { version = \"0.4.2\", optional = true }\ndifferential-dataflow = { version = \"0.18.0\", optional = true }\nderivative = { version = \"2.2.0\" }\neither = \"1.15.0\"\nfutures = { version = \"0.3.31\", optional = true }\nhibitset = { version = \"0.6.4\", optional = true }\nitertools = \"0.14.0\"\nlgalloc = { version = \"0.6\", optional = true }\nlibc = { version = \"0.2.180\", optional = true }\nmz-ore-proc = { path = \"../ore-proc\", default-features = false }\nnum = \"0.4.3\"\nnum-traits = { version = \"0.2\", optional = true }\n# The vendored feature is transitively depended upon by tokio-openssl.\nopenssl = { version = \"0.10.75\", features = [\"vendored\"], optional = true }\nparquet = { version = \"57\", optional = true, default-features = false }\npaste = \"1.0.11\"\npin-project = \"1.1.10\"\nprometheus = { version = \"0.14.0\", default-features = false, optional = true }\nproptest = { version = \"1.9.0\", default-features = false, features = [\"std\"], optional = true }",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./doc/developer/guide.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 139
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 186
          }
        },
        "contents": "script, which constructs a local virtual environment and keeps necessary\ndependencies up to date.\n\nWe support, as a minimum version, the default Python provided in the [most\nrecent Ubuntu LTS release](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/Releases). As of October 2023\nthis is Python 3.10, provided in Ubuntu \"Jammy Jellyfish\". Earlier versions may\nwork but are not supported. Our recommended installation methods are:\n\n- macOS: [Homebrew](https://brew.sh)\n- Linux: System package manager if possible, or [community package repositories](https://launchpad.net/~deadsnakes/+archive/ubuntu/ppa) if necessary\n- Windows: [Microsoft App Store](https://apps.microsoft.com/detail/python-3-11/9NRWMJP3717K?hl=en-US&amp;gl=US)\n\nIf none of the above work well for you, these are a few other methods that have\nworked for us in the past, but are not formally supported:\n\n- [pyenv](https://github.com/pyenv/pyenv)\n- [asdf](https://asdf-vm.com/)\n- [Pixi](https://github.com/prefix-dev/pixi)\n- [Conda](https://docs.conda.io/en/latest/)\n\n### Confluent Platform\n\nThe [Confluent Platform] bundles [Apache ZooKeeper] and [Apache Kafka] with\nseveral non-free Confluent tools, like the [Confluent Schema Registry] and\n[Control Center]. For local development, the [Confluent CLI] allows easy\nmanagement of these services.\n\n**Confluent Platform is not required for changes that don't need Kafka\nintegration**. If your changes don't affect integration with external systems\nand can be fully exercised by SQL logic tests, we recommend not installing the\nConfluent Platform, as it is a rather heavy dependency. Most Materialize\nemployees, or other major contributors, will probably need to run the full test\nsuite and should therefore install the Confluent Platform.\n\n\n#### All platforms\n\nFirst, install the CLI. As of early July 2022 you can run this command on\nmacOS and Linux:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;shell\ncurl -sL --http1.1 https://cnfl.io/cli | sudo sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin latest\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf this no longer works, follow the instructions in the [Confluent CLI]\ndocumentation. Then please update this guide with the new instructions!",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "script, which constructs a local virtual environment and keeps necessary",
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "text": "dependencies up to date.",
            "lineNumber": 141
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142
          },
          {
            "text": "We support, as a minimum version, the default Python provided in the [most",
            "lineNumber": 143
          },
          {
            "text": "recent Ubuntu LTS release](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/Releases). As of October 2023",
            "lineNumber": 144
          },
          {
            "text": "this is Python 3.10, provided in Ubuntu \"Jammy Jellyfish\". Earlier versions may",
            "lineNumber": 145
          },
          {
            "text": "work but are not supported. Our recommended installation methods are:",
            "lineNumber": 146
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147
          },
          {
            "text": "- macOS: [Homebrew](https://brew.sh)",
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "text": "- Linux: System package manager if possible, or [community package repositories](https://launchpad.net/~deadsnakes/+archive/ubuntu/ppa) if necessary",
            "lineNumber": 149
          },
          {
            "text": "- Windows: [Microsoft App Store](https://apps.microsoft.com/detail/python-3-11/9NRWMJP3717K?hl=en-US&amp;gl=US)",
            "lineNumber": 150
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151
          },
          {
            "text": "If none of the above work well for you, these are a few other methods that have",
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {
            "text": "worked for us in the past, but are not formally supported:",
            "lineNumber": 153
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154
          },
          {
            "text": "- [pyenv](https://github.com/pyenv/pyenv)",
            "lineNumber": 155
          },
          {
            "text": "- [asdf](https://asdf-vm.com/)",
            "lineNumber": 156
          },
          {
            "text": "- [Pixi](https://github.com/prefix-dev/pixi)",
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "text": "- [Conda](https://docs.conda.io/en/latest/)",
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159
          },
          {
            "text": "### Confluent Platform",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "The [Confluent Platform] bundles [Apache ZooKeeper] and [Apache Kafka] with",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "text": "several non-free Confluent tools, like the [Confluent Schema Registry] and",
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "[Control Center]. For local development, the [Confluent CLI] allows easy",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "text": "management of these services.",
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166
          },
          {
            "text": "**Confluent Platform is not required for changes that don't need Kafka",
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "text": "integration**. If your changes don't affect integration with external systems",
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "text": "and can be fully exercised by SQL logic tests, we recommend not installing the",
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "text": "Confluent Platform, as it is a rather heavy dependency. Most Materialize",
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "employees, or other major contributors, will probably need to run the full test",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "suite and should therefore install the Confluent Platform.",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "#### All platforms",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "text": "First, install the CLI. As of early July 2022 you can run this command on",
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "text": "macOS and Linux:",
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;shell",
            "lineNumber": 180
          },
          {
            "text": "curl -sL --http1.1 https://cnfl.io/cli | sudo sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin latest",
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "text": "If this no longer works, follow the instructions in the [Confluent CLI]",
            "lineNumber": 184
          },
          {
            "text": "documentation. Then please update this guide with the new instructions!",
            "lineNumber": 185
          }
        ]

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/cloud-resources/Cargo.toml",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 45
          }
        },
        "contents": "[package]\nname = \"mz-cloud-resources\"\ndescription = \"Controller for cloud resources.\"\nversion = \"0.0.0\"\nedition.workspace = true\nrust-version.workspace = true\npublish = false\n\n[lints]\nworkspace = true\n\n[[bin]]\nname = \"crd-writer\"\npath = \"src/bin/crd_writer.rs\"\n\n[dependencies]\nanyhow = \"1.0.100\"\nchrono = { version = \"0.4.39\", default-features = false }\nfutures = \"0.3.31\"\nindexmap = { version = \"2.10.0\", default-features = false, features = [\"std\"] }\nk8s-openapi = { version = \"0.27.0\", features = [\"schemars\", \"v1_32\"] }\nkube = { version = \"3.0.0\", default-features = false, features = [\"client\", \"derive\", \"openssl-tls\", \"ws\", \"runtime\"] }\nmz-ore = { path = \"../ore\", default-features = false, features = [\"async\"] }\nmz-server-core = { path = \"../server-core\", default-features = false }\nrand = \"0.9.2\"\nschemars = { version = \"1.1.0\", features = [\"uuid1\"] }\nsemver = \"1.0.27\"\nserde = \"1.0.219\"\nserde_json = \"1.0.149\"\ntracing = \"0.1.44\"\nuuid = { version = \"1.19\", features = [\"serde\", \"v4\"] }\nworkspace-hack = { version = \"0.0.0\", path = \"../workspace-hack\", optional = true }\n\nasync-trait = { version = \"0.1.89\", optional = true }\nmz-repr = { path = \"../repr\", default-features = false, optional = true }\n\n[dev-dependencies]\nmz-ore = { path = \"../ore\", default-features = false, features = [\"test\"] }\n\n[features]\ndefault = [\"workspace-hack\", \"vpc-endpoints\"]\nvpc-endpoints = [\"async-trait\", \"mz-repr\"]\n\n[package.metadata.cargo-udeps.ignore]\nnormal = [\"workspace-hack\"]",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

        "contents": "[package]\nname = \"mz\"\ndescription = \"The Materialize command-line interface (CLI).\"\nlicense = \"Apache-2.0\"\nversion = \"0.3.1\"\nedition.workspace = true\nrust-version.workspace = true\n\n[lints]\nworkspace = true\n\n[dependencies]\naxum = \"0.7.5\"\nclap = { version = \"4.5.23\", features = [ \"derive\" ] }\ncsv = \"1.4.0\"\ndirs = \"6.0.0\"\nindicatif = \"0.18.3\"\ntermcolor = \"1.4.1\"\nmaplit = \"1.0.2\"\nmz-cloud-api = { path = \"../cloud-api\" }\nmz-frontegg-client = { path = \"../frontegg-client\" }\nmz-frontegg-auth = { path = \"../frontegg-auth\" }\nmz-build-info = { path = \"../build-info\" }\nmz-ore = { path = \"../ore\", features = [\"async\", \"cli\", \"test\"] }\nopen = \"5.3.3\"\nopenssl-probe = \"0.1.6\"\nhyper = \"1.4.1\"\nreqwest = { version = \"0.12.28\", features = [\"blocking\", \"json\", \"default-tls\", \"charset\", \"http2\"], default-features = false }\nrpassword = \"7.4.0\"\nsemver = \"1.0.27\"\nserde = { version = \"1.0.219\", features = [\"derive\"] }\nserde-aux = \"4.7.0\"\nserde_json = \"1.0.149\"\ntabled = \"0.20.0\"\ntime = \"0.3.17\"\ntokio = { version = \"1.48.0\", features = [\"full\"] }\ntoml = \"0.8.22\"\ntoml_edit = { version = \"0.22.26\", features = [\"serde\"] }\nthiserror = \"2.0.18\"\nuuid = \"1.19.0\"\nurl = \"2.5.8\"\nworkspace-hack = { version = \"0.0.0\", path = \"../workspace-hack\", optional = true }\n\n[dev-dependencies]\nassert_cmd = \"2.1.2\"\n\n[target.'cfg(target_os = \"macos\")'.dependencies]\nsecurity-framework = \"2.10.0\"\n\n[package.metadata.cargo-udeps.ignore]\nnormal = [\"workspace-hack\"]\n\n[package.metadata.deb]\nname = \"materialize-cli\"\nmaintainer = \"Materialize, Inc.\"\ncopyright = \"Materialize, Inc. and contributors\"\nsection = \"utility\"\nassets = [\n    [\"target/release/mz\", \"usr/bin/\", \"755\"],\n]\ndepends = \"postgresql-client, $auto\"\n\n[features]\ndefault = [\"workspace-hack\"]",

        "contents": "[package]\nname = \"mz-interchange\"\ndescription = \"Translations for various data serialization formats.\"\nversion = \"0.0.0\"\nedition.workspace = true\nrust-version.workspace = true\npublish = false\n\n[lints]\nworkspace = true\n\n[[bench]]\nname = \"mz-interchange-bench\"\npath = \"benches/benches.rs\"\nharness = false\n\n[dependencies]\nanyhow = \"1.0.100\"\nbyteorder = \"1.4.3\"\nbytes = \"1.10.1\"\nchrono = { version = \"0.4.39\", default-features = false, features = [\"std\"] }\nclap = { version = \"4.5.23\", features = [\"derive\"] }\ndifferential-dataflow = \"0.18.0\"\nitertools = \"0.14.0\"\nmaplit = \"1.0.2\"\nmz-avro = { path = \"../avro\", features = [\"snappy\"] }\nmz-ccsr = { path = \"../ccsr\" }\nmz-ore = { path = \"../ore\", features = [\"network\", \"cli\"] }\nmz-repr = { path = \"../repr\" }\nmz-pgrepr = { path = \"../pgrepr\" }\nordered-float = { version = \"5.1.0\", features = [\"serde\"] }\nprost = { version = \"0.13.5\", features = [\"no-recursion-limit\"] }\nprost-reflect = \"0.15.3\"\nseahash = \"4\"\nserde = \"1.0.219\"\nserde_json = \"1.0.149\"\ntimely = \"0.25.1\"\ntokio = { version = \"1.48.0\", features = [\"macros\", \"net\", \"rt\", \"rt-multi-thread\", \"time\"] }\ntracing = \"0.1.44\"\nuuid = { version = \"1.19.0\", features = [\"serde\"] }\nworkspace-hack = { version = \"0.0.0\", path = \"../workspace-hack\", optional = true }\n\n[dev-dependencies]\ncriterion = { version = \"0.8.1\", features = [\"async_tokio\"] }\ntokio = { version = \"1.48.0\", features = [\"macros\"] }\n\n[build-dependencies]\nmz-build-tools = { path = \"../build-tools\", default-features = false, features = [\"protobuf-src\"] }\nprost-build = \"0.13.5\"\n\n[features]\ndefault = [\"mz-build-tools/default\", \"workspace-hack\"]\n\n[package.metadata.cargo-udeps.ignore]\nnormal = [\"workspace-hack\"]",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./ci/test/lint-main/checks/check-python-version.sh",
        "range": {

        "contents": "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n\n# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n#\n# Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n# included in the LICENSE file at the root of this repository.\n#\n# As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n# the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n# by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n#\n# check-python-version.sh — make sure Python 3.10 keeps working\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\ncd \"$(dirname \"$0\")/../../../..\"\n\n. misc/shlib/shlib.bash\n\nif [[ ! \"${MZDEV_NO_PYTHON:-}\" ]]; then\n    if ! uv --version &gt;/dev/null 2&gt;/dev/null; then\n        echo \"lint: uv is not installed\"\n        echo \"hint: refer to https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/ for install instructions\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\n\n    try uv venv --python 3.10\n    try uv pip compile --python-version 3.10 ci/builder/requirements.txt\n    try uv pip install --python 3.10 --requirement ci/builder/requirements.txt\n    try git ls-files '*.py' | xargs uv run --no-project --python 3.10 -- python -m compileall -q\nfi\n\ntry_status_report",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "# check-python-version.sh — make sure Python 3.10 keeps working",

Specifically, `PyUnicode_New` and `PyUnicode_KIND` functions have been removed in Python 3.14, causing compilation failures in jiter (a dependency) when building against Python 3.14.[3] This has resulted in build failures for projects depending on pydantic-core on Python 3.14.[5]<br/><br/>## PyO3 Migration Plans<br/><br/>The pydantic-core project has identified a need to upgrade PyO3 to support free-threaded Python (PEP 703). Key blockers for migrating to PyO3 0.23+ include:[2]<br/><br/>- Jiter support for free-threaded Python<br/>- Making `SerializationCallable` thread-safe (blocked by `RefCell` usage in `CollectWarnings` and `SerRecursionState`)<br/>- Replacing deprecated `IntoPy`/`ToPyObject` APIs with `IntoPyObject`<br/>- Updating calls to deprecated bound methods and `into_py`/`to_object` functions<br/><br/>## Current Status<br/><br/>Pydantic-core v2.41.5 includes CI updates for Python 3.14 builds,[4] suggesting ongoing work to address compatibility issues, though the compilation errors with Python 3.14 appear to still need resolution through a PyO3 upgrade and jiter updates. |

        "contents": "script, which constructs a local virtual environment and keeps necessary\ndependencies up to date.\n\nWe support, as a minimum version, the default Python provided in the [most\nrecent Ubuntu LTS release](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/Releases). As of October 2023\nthis is Python 3.10, provided in Ubuntu \"Jammy Jellyfish\". Earlier versions may\nwork but are not supported. Our recommended installation methods are:\n\n- macOS: [Homebrew](https://brew.sh)\n- Linux: System package manager if possible, or [community package repositories](https://launchpad.net/~deadsnakes/+archive/ubuntu/ppa) if necessary\n- Windows: [Microsoft App Store](https://apps.microsoft.com/detail/python-3-11/9NRWMJP3717K?hl=en-US&amp;gl=US)\n\nIf none of the above work well for you, these are a few other methods that have\nworked for us in the past, but are not formally supported:\n\n- [pyenv](https://github.com/pyenv/pyenv)\n- [asdf](https://asdf-vm.com/)\n- [Pixi](https://github.com/prefix-dev/pixi)\n- [Conda](https://docs.conda.io/en/latest/)\n\n### Confluent Platform\n\nThe [Confluent Platform] bundles [Apache ZooKeeper] and [Apache Kafka] with\nseveral non-free Confluent tools, like the [Confluent Schema Registry] and\n[Control Center]. For local development, the [Confluent CLI] allows easy\nmanagement of these services.\n\n**Confluent Platform is not required for changes that don't need Kafka\nintegration**. If your changes don't affect integration with external systems\nand can be fully exercised by SQL logic tests, we recommend not installing the\nConfluent Platform, as it is a rather heavy dependency. Most Materialize\nemployees, or other major contributors, will probably need to run the full test\nsuite and should therefore install the Confluent Platform.\n\n\n#### All platforms\n\nFirst, install the CLI. As of early July 2022 you can run this command on\nmacOS and Linux:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;shell\ncurl -sL --http1.1 https://cnfl.io/cli | sudo sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin latest\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf this no longer works, follow the instructions in the [Confluent CLI]\ndocumentation. Then please update this guide with the new instructions!",

        "contents": "# Packages required for Python developer tools.\n#\n# Please be thoughtful about adding dependencies. Packages with native\n# dependencies tend to be troublesome unless they ship binary\n# wheels for a wide variety of platforms, including M1 Macs.\n\nblack==24.4.2\n# Pulls in dependencies that make       "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./test/console/README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 91
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Introduction\n\nThis directory contains mzcompose workflows for testing the Materialize console\nagainst different Materialize versions.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Start services with the default (current source) build\n./mzcompose run default\n\n# Run console SQL tests (from console repo)\ncd ~/dev/console &amp;&amp; yarn test:sql\n\n# Stop services when done\ncd ~/dev/materialize/test/console &amp;&amp; ./mzcompose down -v\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Available Workflows\n\n### &#96;default&#96; - Start Services (Current Source)\n\nStarts Materialize (current source build) with Redpanda, Postgres, MySQL, and SQL Server.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n./mzcompose run default\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### &#96;list-versions&#96; - Show Version Matrix\n\nOutputs a JSON object with the versions to test against. This is useful for CI/CD\npipelines that need to determine which versions to test.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n./mzcompose run list-versions\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Example output:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"latest\": \"v26.1.1\",\n  \"previous\": \"v26.0.0\",\n  \"older\": \"v0.164.2\",\n  \"self-managed\": \"v26.1.1\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Version meanings:**\n- &#96;latest&#96;: Most recent minor version\n- &#96;previous&#96;: One minor version back\n- &#96;older&#96;: Two minor versions back\n- &#96;self-managed&#96;: Latest self-managed version from Helm chart\n\n### &#96;start-version&#96; - Start Services with Specific Version\n\nStarts services with a specific Materialize version. Use this for testing console\ncompatibility across different Materialize releases.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Using version aliases\n./mzcompose run start-version latest\n./mzcompose run start-version previous\n./mzcompose run start-version older\n./mzcompose run start-version self-managed\n\n# Using a direct version tag\n./mzcompose run start-version v26.1.1\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAfter starting, run tests from the console repo:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ncd ~/dev/console &amp;&amp; yarn test:sql\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen stop services:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n./mzcompose down -v\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Multi-Version Testing (Local)\n\nTo test against all versions locally:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# 1. See available versions\n./mzcompose run list-versions\n\n# 2. Test each version\nfor version in latest previous older self-managed; do\n  echo \"Testing $version...\"\n  ./mzcompose down -v",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./misc/python/materialize/console_version_matrix.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 57,
            "column": 5
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n#\n# Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n# included in the LICENSE file at the root of this repository.\n#\n# As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n# the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n# by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n\"\"\"\nVersion matrix for console testing - uses existing version_list infrastructure.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom materialize.mz_version import MzVersion\nfrom materialize.version_list import (\n    get_published_minor_mz_versions,\n    get_self_managed_versions,\n)\n\n\ndef get_console_test_versions() -&gt; dict[str, MzVersion]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Get all versions that should be tested for console.\n\n    Uses existing version_list.py infrastructure to determine:\n    - latest: Most recent minor version\n    - previous: One minor version back\n    - older: Two minor versions back\n    - self-managed: Latest self-managed version from Helm chart\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary mapping test name to version\n\n    Note: Uses get_published_minor_mz_versions which verifies Docker images exist.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Get the latest patch version for each minor version (with Docker image verification)\n    # Limit to 5 versions for speed\n    minor_versions = get_published_minor_mz_versions(newest_first=True, limit=5)\n\n    assert (\n        len(minor_versions) &gt;= 3\n    ), f\"Expected at least 3 minor versions, got {len(minor_versions)}\"\n\n    latest = minor_versions[0]\n    previous = minor_versions[1]\n    older = minor_versions[2]\n\n    # Self-managed: Get the latest self-managed version from Helm chart\n    sm_versions = get_self_managed_versions()\n    assert sm_versions, \"Expected at least one self-managed version\"\n    self_managed = max(sm_versions)\n\n    return {\n        \"latest\": latest,\n        \"previous\": previous,\n        \"older\": older,\n        \"self-managed\": self_managed,\n    }",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "#",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "# Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "# included in the LICENSE file at the root of this repository.",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "#",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "# As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "# the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "# by the Apache License, Version 2.0.",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "Version matrix for console testing - uses existing version_list infrastructure.",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "from materialize.mz_version import MzVersion",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "from materialize.version_list import (",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "    get_published_minor_mz_versions,",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "    get_self_managed_versions,",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": ")",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "def get_console_test_versions() -&gt; dict[str, MzVersion]:",
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    Get all versions that should be tested for console.",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    Uses existing version_list.py infrastructure to determine:",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "    - latest: Most recent minor version",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "    - previous: One minor version back",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    - older: Two minor versions back",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    - self-managed: Latest self-managed version from Helm chart",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "        Dictionary mapping test name to version",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "    Note: Uses get_published_minor_mz_versions which verifies Docker images exist.",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Get the latest patch version for each minor version (with Docker image verification)",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Limit to 5 versions for speed",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "    minor_versions = get_published_minor_mz_versions(newest_first=True, limit=5)",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "    assert (",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "        len(minor_versions) &gt;= 3",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    ), f\"Expected at least 3 minor versions, got {len(minor_versions)}\"",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    latest = minor_versions[0]",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "    previous = minor_versions[1]",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "    older = minor_versions[2]",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Self-managed: Get the latest self-managed version from Helm chart",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "    sm_versions = get_self_managed_versions()",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "    assert sm_versions, \"Expected at least one self-managed version\"",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    self_managed = max(sm_versions)",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "    return {",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"latest\": latest,",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"previous\": previous,",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"older\": older,",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"self-managed\": self_managed,",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.36473241448402405
    },

        "contents": "# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n#\n# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n# You may obtain a copy of the License in the LICENSE file at the\n# root of this repository, or online at\n#\n#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n#\n# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n# distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n# limitations under the License.\n\n[project]\nname = \"mcp-materialize\"\nversion = \"0.3.1\"\ndescription = \"A server that exposes Materialize indexes as tools over the Model Context Protocol (MCP)\"\nreadme = \"README.md\"\nrequires-python = \"&gt;=3.13\"\nauthors = [\n    {name = \"Materialize Inc\", email = \"support@materialize.com\"}\n]\n\nkeywords = [\"materialize\", \"mcp\", \"database\", \"api\"]\ndependencies = [\n    \"aiohttp&gt;=3.11.18\",\n    \"aiorwlock&gt;=1.5.0\",\n    \"fastapi&gt;=0.115.12\",\n    \"mcp&gt;=1.10.1\",\n    \"psycopg-pool&gt;=3.2.6\",\n    \"psycopg[binary]&gt;=3.2.6\",\n    \"pytest&gt;=8.4.1\",\n    \"pytest-asyncio&gt;=1.0.0\",\n    \"pyyaml&gt;=6.0.2\",\n    \"requests&gt;=2.32.3\",\n]\n\n[project.scripts]\nmcp-materialize = \"mcp_materialize:main\"\n\n[build-system]\nrequires = [\"hatchling\"]\nbuild-backend = \"hatchling.build\"\n\n[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]\npackages = [\"mcp_materialize\"]\n\n[tool.hatch.build]\n\n[dependency-groups]\ndev = [\n    \"pytest-asyncio&gt;=0.26.0\",\n    \"pytest&gt;=8.3.5\",\n    \"hatch&gt;=1.9.4\"\n]\n\n[tool.ruff]\nline-length = 88\nlint.select = [\"I\", \"UP\", \"F\", \"E\"]\nlint.extend-select = [\"E501\"]",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./doc/developer/guide.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 72
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Developer guide\n\nThis guide details what you'll need to contribute to Materialize.\n\nMaterialize consists of several services written in [Rust] that are orchestrated\nby [Kubernetes]. Supporting build and test tools are written in a combination of\nRust, [Python], and [Bash]. Tests often use [Docker Compose] rather than\nKubernetes to orchestrate interactions with other systems, like [Apache Kafka].\n\n## Install build and test dependencies\n\n### C components\n\nMaterialize depends on several components that are written in C and C++, so\nyou'll need a working C and C++ toolchain. You'll also need to install:\n* The [CMake] build system\n* libclang\n* PostgreSQL\n* lld (on Linux, or set a custom &#96;RUSTFLAGS&#96;)\n\nOn macOS, if you install [Homebrew], you'll be guided through the process of\ninstalling Apple's developer tools, which includes a C compiler and libclang.\nThen it's a cinch to install CMake and PostgreSQL.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nbrew install cmake postgresql\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOn Debian-based Linux variants, it's even easier:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;shell\nsudo apt update\nsudo apt install build-essential cmake postgresql-client libclang-dev lld\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOn other platforms, you'll have to figure out how to get these tools yourself.\n\n### Rust\n\nInstall Rust via [rustup]:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;shell\ncurl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWe recommend that you do _not_ install Rust via your system's package manager.\nWe closely track the most recent version of Rust. The version of Rust in your\npackage manager is likely too old to build Materialize.\n\nFor details on how we upgrade Rust see [here](/doc/developer/upgrade-rust.md).\n\n### Docker\n\nMaterialize's tests mostly require Docker and Docker Compose to be installed. On macOS it is part of Docker Desktop:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;shell\nbrew install docker\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOn Debian-based Linux both Docker and the Docker Compose plugin have to be installed:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;shell\nsudo apt update\nsudo apt install docker docker-compose-plugin\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Metadata store\n\nRunning Materialize locally requires a running Postgres / CockroachDB server.\n\nOn macOS, when using Homebrew, CockroachDB can be installed and started via:",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Developer guide",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "This guide details what you'll need to contribute to Materialize.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "Materialize consists of several services written in [Rust] that are orchestrated",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "by [Kubernetes]. Supporting build and test tools are written in a combination of",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "Rust, [Python], and [Bash]. Tests often use [Docker Compose] rather than",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "Kubernetes to orchestrate interactions with other systems, like [Apache Kafka].",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "## Install build and test dependencies",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "### C components",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "Materialize depends on several components that are written in C and C++, so",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "you'll need a working C and C++ toolchain. You'll also need to install:",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "* The [CMake] build system",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "* libclang",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "* PostgreSQL",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "* lld (on Linux, or set a custom &#96;RUSTFLAGS&#96;)",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "On macOS, if you install [Homebrew], you'll be guided through the process of",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "installing Apple's developer tools, which includes a C compiler and libclang.",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "Then it's a cinch to install CMake and PostgreSQL.",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "brew install cmake postgresql",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "On Debian-based Linux variants, it's even easier:",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;shell",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "sudo apt update",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "sudo apt install build-essential cmake postgresql-client libclang-dev lld",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "On other platforms, you'll have to figure out how to get these tools yourself.",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "### Rust",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "Install Rust via [rustup]:",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;shell",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "We recommend that you do _not_ install Rust via your system's package manager.",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "We closely track the most recent version of Rust. The version of Rust in your",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "package manager is likely too old to build Materialize.",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "For details on how we upgrade Rust see [here](/doc/developer/upgrade-rust.md).",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "### Docker",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "Materialize's tests mostly require Docker and Docker Compose to be installed. On macOS it is part of Docker Desktop:",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;shell",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "brew install docker",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "On Debian-based Linux both Docker and the Docker Compose plugin have to be installed:",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;shell",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "sudo apt update",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "sudo apt install docker docker-compose-plugin",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "### Metadata store",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "Running Materialize locally requires a running Postgres / CockroachDB server.",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "On macOS, when using Homebrew, CockroachDB can be installed and started via:",
            "lineNumber": 71

        "contents": "# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n#\n# Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n# included in the LICENSE file at the root of this repository.\n#\n# As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n# the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n# by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n\"\"\"\nTest various Confluent Platform d Redpanda versions to make sure they are all\nworking with Materialize.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom materialize import buildkite\nfrom materialize.mzcompose import DEFAULT_CONFLUENT_PLATFORM_VERSION\nfrom materialize.mzcompose.composition import Composition\nfrom materialize.mzcompose.services.kafka import Kafka\nfrom materialize.mzcompose.services.localstack import Localstack\nfrom materialize.mzcompose.services.materialized import Materialized\nfrom materialize.mzcompose.services.redpanda import REDPANDA_VERSION, Redpanda\nfrom materialize.mzcompose.services.schema_registry import SchemaRegistry\nfrom materialize.mzcompose.services.testdrive import Testdrive\nfrom materialize.mzcompose.services.zookeeper import Zookeeper\n\nREDPANDA_VERSIONS = [\n    \"v22.3.25\",\n    \"v23.1.21\",\n    \"v23.2.29\",\n    \"v23.3.21\",\n    \"v24.1.21\",\n    \"v24.2.27\",\n    \"v24.3.18\",\n    \"v25.1.12\",\n    REDPANDA_VERSION,\n    \"latest\",\n]\n\nCONFLUENT_PLATFORM_VERSIONS = [\n    \"7.0.16\",\n    \"7.1.16\",\n    \"7.2.14\",\n    \"7.3.12\",\n    \"7.4.12\",\n    \"7.5.11\",\n    \"7.6.8\",\n    \"7.7.6\",\n    \"7.8.5\",\n    DEFAULT_CONFLUENT_PLATFORM_VERSION,\n    # There is currently a mismatch in the latest versions between cp-zookeeper\n    # (7.9.4) and cp-kafka (8.0.0), making running them in combination\n    # impossible\n    # \"latest\",\n]\n\nSERVICES = [\n    Materialized(default_replication_factor=2),\n    # Occasional timeouts in CI with 60s timeout\n    Testdrive(\n        volumes_extra=[\"../testdrive:/workdir/testdrive\"], default_timeout=\"120s\"\n    ),\n    Redpanda(),\n    Zookeeper(),\n    Kafka(),\n    SchemaRegistry(),\n    Localstack(),\n]\n\n\nTD_CMD = [\n    f\"--var=default-replica-size=scale=",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./test/legacy-upgrade/README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 66
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Legacy framework !\n\nThis framework is considered legacy. While it continues to run in CI, it is recommended that\nnew functionality is tested using Platform Checks in test/platform-checks. Any Check that\nis defined in that framework will be run by the CI in various upgrade contexts as well.\n\n# testdrive-based upgrade test framework\n\nThis upgrade test framework serves to verify that objects created in a previous version of materialize are still operational after an upgrade.\n\n## Mode of operation\n\nThe framework does the following:\n- fires an old version of Materialize\n- runs the applicable 'create-in' .td tests against it\n- kills the old version and starts a Materialize binary from your current source, preserving the mzdata directory across restarts\n- runs the applicable 'check-from' .td tests\n\nThe external services (Kafka, Schema Registry, Postgres) are not restarted.\n\nThe \"upgrade\" from your current source to your current source is also tested.\n\n## Running\n\nTo run the entire sequence of tests:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./mzcompose down -v ; ./mzcompose run default\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTo run the tests against a particular version and all following versions:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./mzcompose down -v ; ./mzcompose run default --min-version 0.9.6\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTo run the tests against the last five versions:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./mzcompose down -v ; ./mzcompose run default --most-recent 5\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTo run the tests upgrading from the current source to the current source:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./mzcompose down -v ; ./mzcompose run default --most-recent 0\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTo run just a particular test or tests:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./mzcompose down -v ; ./mzcompose run default 'avro-*'\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf you are running a particular test that specifies the version, then you\nmust include the &#96;--min-version&#96; flag with that version. The reason is\nthat the test won't be included when testing earlier versions of Materialize,\nand testdrive will error out if it has no files to test:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n./mzcompose down -v ; ./mzcompose run default 'compile-proto*' --min-version 0.9.12\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nFor a full description of the command line options, run:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "lineNumber": 5

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./misc/fivetran-sdk/.github/workflows/audit.yml",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 73
          }
        },
        "contents": "name: Audit\n\non:\n  push:\n    branches: [main]\n  pull_request:\n    branches: [main]\n    types: [ opened, labeled, unlabeled, synchronize, reopened, edited ]\n\njobs:\n\n  audit:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: read\n      pull-requests: read\n      issues: read\n      packages: none\n    env:\n      GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}\n    steps:\n    - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n    - uses: actions/setup-java@v4\n      with:\n        distribution: 'zulu'\n        java-version: '17'\n\n    - name: Install Python\n      uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n\n    - name: Install Python dependencies\n      run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel\n\n    - name: Install Python connector requirements\n      working-directory: examples/source_connector/python\n      run: pip install -r requirements.txt\n\n    - name: Run Python connector build script\n      working-directory: examples/source_connector/python\n      run: ./build.sh\n\n    - name: Install Python destination requirements\n      working-directory: examples/destination_connector/python\n      run: pip install -r requirements.txt\n\n    - name: Run Python destination build script\n      working-directory: examples/destination_connector/python\n      run: ./build.sh\n\n    - name: Set up Go\n      uses: actions/setup-go@v4\n      with:\n        go-version: 1.22\n\n    - name: Install Protoc\n      uses: arduino/setup-protoc@v3\n      with:\n        version: \"23.2\"\n\n    - name: Set up protobuf for Go\n      working-directory: examples/source_connector/golang\n      run: go install google.golang.org/protobuf/cmd/protoc-gen-go@latest\n\n    - name: Set up protoc-gen-go\n      working-directory: examples/source_connector/golang\n      run: go install google.golang.org/grpc/cmd/protoc-gen-go-grpc@latest\n\n    - name: Set up Go protoc path\n      working-directory: examples/source_connector/golang\n      run: export PATH=\"$PATH:$(go env GOPATH)/bin\"\n\n    - name: Verify dependencies\n      working-directory: examples/source_connector/golang",

---


The project targets Python 3.10–3.12, not 3.14. Evidence:

1. The developer guide says: "We support, as a minimum version, the default Python provided in the most recent Ubuntu LTS release... this is Python 3.10"
2. CI explicitly tests Python 3.10: `check-python-version.sh` runs tests with Python 3.10
3. Requirements are pinned for Python 3.10 compatibility (e.g., "Keep on 3.4.2 for Python 3.10 compatibility")

Why it doesn't work with Python 3.14:
- Python 3.14 is very new (released October 2024)
- Many packages don't have Python 3.14 wheels yet
- The pinned versions were chosen for Python 3.10 compatibility and stability

The trade-off: supporting the latest Python vs. maintaining compatibility with older versions.

Options:
1. Use Python 3.12 (recommended) — well-supported and stable
2. Continue with Python 3.14 — we can update dependencies, but you may hit more issues as packages catch up

Should I finish updating the remaining dependencies for Python 3.14, or would you prefer to switch to Python 3.12?
